item.title: Curl cheatsheet
item.description: Curl is an invaluable tool for anyone who needs to debug webapps fequently. Given it’s vast amount of options, it is easy to miss some of it’s simplest yet powerful uses. Let me add a few here, and I’ll keep updating it as and when I discover something new! 🤘 Inspect response headers The simplest way to do that is by using the -I option - $ curl -I https://httpbin.org/ip HTTP/2 200 date: Thu, 12 Nov 2019 19:08:13 GMT content-type: application/json content-length: 32 server: gunicorn/19.

item.title: Elasticsearch Ingest Pipelines
item.description: Elasticsearch is an open-source search solution which is quite popular for centralzed logs ingestion. It allows logs from various different sources to be available and searchable at a single place. Ingesting data from various sources creates a problem. How do you normalize the incoming data, split it into some common fields, add or remove metadata etc. To perform all of this (and more), Logstash is the go-to solution. It can manipulate, transform incoming data before pushing it off to Elasticsearch for indexing.
